item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you.

And now it's even MORE horrifying.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded: I'd prefer it link to SCP-3663 (I don't really like how 3617 turned out), but other than that it seems like a good idea. Heck, I'm gonna go and add it in myself!

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
Edits made by author.

The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This article mostly stands on it's own, and also occupies a rather unique position within the site (there are no articles with both the hostile and appliance tags!) but it does mention Site-54, which has been frequently mentioned by MaliceAF in his works.

As such, I recommend crosslinking to another SCP set there:

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-2856 has been acquired by the Foundation, and is currently operated by "Storage Concepts plc." (a Foundation front company) under the pretence of non-perishable goods storage. At least six personnel must remain within SCP-2856 at all times, with no fewer than three housed there permanently. Due to the proximity of SCP-2856 to Site-54, plans to outfit the location as a rapid-response vehicle hangar and low-value storage unit are currently undergoing approval.

To:

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-2856 has been acquired by the Foundation, and is currently operated by "Storage Concepts plc." (a Foundation front company) under the pretence of non-perishable goods storage. At least six personnel must remain within SCP-2856 at all times, with no fewer than three housed there permanently. Due to the proximity of SCP-2856 to Site-54, plans to outfit the location as a rapid-response vehicle hangar and low-value storage unit are currently undergoing approval.

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Viva la revolución all rally to the toaster rebellion +1

item.title: +1.0e2!
item.content:encoded:
[deep breath]

[INCOHERENT SCREAMING]

My first scip to reach +100! I honestly can't believe it — I love you guys! All of you, whether you upvoted or not. Words cannot express how much this means to me, and each and every one of you has a special place in my heart. It's cliche to say "I couldn't have done it without you", but I really, really couldn't. Thanks for everything <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do you mind pointing out some specifics for me? I usually get criticised for being too dull, not overly wordy :P

Also, check out DarkStuff's spoilers if you haven't already: they should clarify some things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept is OK, but it's really, really wordy (I appreciate a broad vocab but in this case it got a bit distracting), and the whole concept seems pretty disjointed - if that's what you were going for then cool, but the hot pieces of metal, chicken soup and 'quiet screaming' killed it for me.

If it weren't so over worded and followed a more linear narrative, it would be a bit easier to take in and more enjoyable.

Not to say the whole idea isn't grim (reminded me of SAW crossed with Silent Hill).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subject expired 20 minutes after manifestation due to the replacement of 20% of all bodily fluids with a fluid resembling chicken soup1.

How does it resemble chicken soup? Colour, consistency, smell, taste?

+1 anyway

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was… surreal. I can think of no other word to describe what this made me feel. This is the sort of stuff that gives the word "anomaly" a run for its money. Excellent work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Lots of effective body horror here.

+ Only Complaint
- Hide

Due to the low possibility of useful employment following this, the subject was terminated 2 days later.

This seems unnecessarily cruel, and sounds a lot like Series I era "Evil Foundation." I think if you simply replaced the above with, "the subject was euthanized 2 days later," it would fit much better with me.

But that's just me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An excellent skip; it manages to maintain an air of depth and mystery throughout, and really ramps up the horror. A great job, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like the brave little toaster as directed by Eli Roth. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this already, but after reading the spoiler darkstuff provided I think it’s damn brilliant. Nice job Malice!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seeing as you didn't even spoil it in your author post, I'll put it under a collapsible here:

Spoiler
Spoiled

It's a power fantasy of the appliances in our lives. Ever had that dream of doing terrible things to your boss? Or heck, to anybody above you in the social food chain? Well, we didn't really think about it, but everything in your house has the same dream. This is a household appliance power fantasy. "Look at you on your fucking high horse, how about you toast the god damn toast for a change and see how you like it you privileged fuck."

Which is why I am still gonna fight for the name A Rallying Cry for the Low-Low-Lower Classes, but it seems you settled on yours. Still, I reserve the right to growl. And on that note, grrr.


This is really, really disturbingly good. Take my updoots. All my updoots.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm still trying to piece together what is happening here but either way it has horrified me. In fact, not knowing what's going on makes it even worse. Nicely done!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am feex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: as i said in the chat, this does a great job at doing what it's trying to do: dip you, just for a moment, into a terrible, surreal, and mysterious place. it's haunting and leaves me wanting more information, wanting to know more about where this thing came from and what the place inside really is. and that's a good thing: the antidote to fear or unease is knowledge, so you've got me reaching for a security blanket, lol. great work, all around. ♥

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are two Experiment 16's in the log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, that's fair. I used it to mean a quiet, hoarse scream: their throat is pretty damaged after all, but I can see the problem. I'll see what I can do about re-wording that.

Edit: Changed "screaming" to a more evocative "moaning", and specified throat-damage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The tone is excellent and the recovered footage is horrifying. Well done.

My only question concerns your use of "screaming quietly." Are you implying the screams are far from the recording device and thus of low volume or should i interpret this as a low moan? If it is the former, I would use a phrase like "screaming in the distance." If it is the later, I wouldn't rely on an adverb but instead find a stronger verb to describe the action.

+1 all the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for the ticking test giving me horrific flashbacks to Serial Experiments Lain.

Beep. Beep. Beep.

item.title: OH MY F*CKING GOD
item.content:encoded:
What ? WHat ? WHAT ?

YOU TWisted, SICK, perverted, f*cked, deviant man.

I love you.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My third SCP, and my darkest yet. It's based near-wholesale on a nightmare I had a few months back, and it's been lurking in my subconscious ever since. Here's hoping people enjoy it!

+ Critique attributions
- Critique attributions

Thanks to…

  • CadaverCommander
  • DarkStuff
  • buckyboy
  • fieldstone
  • Tufto

…for looking this over before I posted it. I couldn't have done it without you!

+ Image attributions
- Image attributions

Image is from here, and is licenced under CC BY-SA 2.0.

Alterations were made using standard GIMP tools.

See DarkStuff's spoilers below for clarification 'n' stuff.

EDIT: We have fanart, people, and good fanart at that. Two pics of 2856 here, along with a bunch of other cool-looking artworks by pastarasta1. If you liked 'em, check out more of their stuff in this thread — it's all very good!

EDIT II: A reading from the newest hip and trendy SCP reader: SkeleJones! Let his deep and masterful voice grace your ears here. While you're at it, check out his other stuff too!
